questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'Sachin is great' is written as 'ga ma ra', 'is he poor' is written as 'ta sa ga', then find the code for 'is'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'fruits are sweet' is written as 'pa ma la' and 'you are sweet' is written as 'pa ma ta', then find the code for 'fruits'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'pick and choose' is written as 'ko ho po' and 'pick up and come' is written as 'to no ko po', then how will 'pick' be written in that language?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'open the door' is written as 'ka ta jo', 'door is closed' is written as 'jo pa ma' and 'this is good' is written as 'la ra pa', then how will 'closed' be written in that language?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: In a certain code language, 'Sue Re Nik' means 'She is brave', 'Pi Sor Re Rik' means 'She is always smiling' and 'Sor Re Zhi' means 'Is always cheerful'. What is the code used for the word 'smiling'?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'nik ka pa' means 'who are you', 'ka na ta da' means 'you may come here' and 'ho ta sa' means 'come and go', then what does 'nik' mean in that language?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If in a certain code language 'how can you go' is written as 'ja da ka pa'; 'you come here' is written as 'na ka sa' and 'come and go' is written as 'ra pa sa', then how will 'here' be written in that language?
